page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors built on the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz with a dedicated allocation and no hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 4 GB of RAM per core, and includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps, and no GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and software compilation via Clang placing in the top 25%. Small-block cached read memory bandwidth is highly efficient at 3792.73 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations reach 79.92 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is relatively weaker, as shown by lower-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and single-core Geekbench scores. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds, along with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, perform in the average range.

Given the dedicated physical cores and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage, the m6gd.metal is well-suited for database operations, redis caching, and static web serving, where it demonstrates high throughput. The ARM64 architecture offers qualitative cost efficiency by delivering high resource density and strong multi-core throughput per unit of power compared to traditional x86 architectures, though workloads requiring x86-specific instruction sets or high single-threaded performance may experience tradeoffs.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by 64 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 256 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, supported by a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The ARM64 architecture delivers strong multi-core performance, particularly in software compilation, Redis operations, and static web serving, where benchmarks place it in the top 25%. However, single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are weaker. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads and high-density deployments, making it highly suitable for database operations, caching layers, and web serving applications that benefit from local high-speed storage and dedicated physical cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.metal is an ARM64 bare-metal server with 64 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 bare-metal platform combining sixty-four dedicated cores with high-capacity local NVMe storage for efficient multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB DDR4 RAM and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong multi-core performance and high-throughput Redis operations
Features 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero hyperthreading overhead
Best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and static web serving
